response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential dangers of misinformation and take steps to verify the accuracy of what you see online. Here are some tips: 
Firstly, be cautious when sharing or liking posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. Fact-checking websites can help you get to the bottom of a story before you hit that share button. 
Secondly, use trusted news sources and fact-checking apps to check the veracity of the content you're viewing. These tools can help you identify fake news and propaganda, and give you a more accurate understanding of current events. 
Thirdly, be mindful of your own online presence and ensure that your social media accounts are secure and protected from hackers. This will help prevent unauthorized access to your personal information and reduce the risk of your account being used to spread false information. 
Finally, stay informed and educated on current events to make better decisions when it comes to what you see and share on social media. By taking these steps, you can effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, and help create a more informed and connected online community.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, be aware of potential dangers of misinformation, fact-check websites, use trusted news sources, protect your online presence, and stay informed on current events.
